summary: Metric CSV file is used for bulk importing and exporting Metric definitions. Use '*' as the {name} path parameter to operate platform-wide, or a Domain fully qualified name to scope import/export to that domain.

headers.name: name
headers.description: Name that uniquely identifies the Metric.

headers.name: description
headers.description: Description of the Metric in Markdown format.
examples: Number of distinct users active in the last 30 days.

headers.name: metricType
headers.description: Type of the Metric. One of: COUNT, SUM, AVERAGE, RATIO, PERCENTAGE, MIN, MAX, MEDIAN, MODE, STANDARD_DEVIATION, VARIANCE, OTHER.

headers.name: unitOfMeasurement
headers.description: Unit of measurement for the Metric. One of: COUNT, DOLLARS, PERCENTAGE, TIMESTAMP, SIZE, REQUESTS, EVENTS, TRANSACTIONS, OTHER.

headers.name: customUnitOfMeasurement
headers.description: Custom unit of measurement when unitOfMeasurement is OTHER.

headers.name: granularity
headers.description: Granularity of the Metric. One of: SECOND, MINUTE, HOUR, DAY, WEEK, MONTH, QUARTER, YEAR.

headers.name: expressionLanguage
headers.description: Language used for the Metric expression code. One of: SQL, Java, JavaScript, Python, DAX, External.

headers.name: expressionCode
headers.description: Metric expression code. For DAX, provide the measure formula (e.g. 'CALCULATE(DISTINCTCOUNT(Users[Id]))').
examples: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM events WHERE event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 day'

headers.name: relatedMetrics
headers.description: Fully qualified names of related Metrics, separated by ';'.

headers.name: owners
headers.description: Owners of the Metric, in the format type:name (e.g. user:alice or team:analytics), separated by ';'.

headers.name: reviewers
headers.description: Reviewers of the Metric, in the format type:name, separated by ';'.

headers.name: tags
headers.description: Fully qualified classification tag names associated with the Metric, separated by ';'.

headers.name: glossaryTerms
headers.description: Fully qualified glossary term names associated with the Metric, separated by ';'.

headers.name: domains
headers.description: Fully qualified names of Domains associated with the Metric, separated by ';'.
